websocket:on-socket-closed.doc:name: On Socket Closed
websocket:on-socket-closed.doc:description: Trigger when Opera closes the connection

java:invoke.doc:name: Read numeric close code
java:invoke.doc:description: Reads the Opera provided socket close code

os:contains.doc:name: Has current socket id?
os:contains.doc:description: Check whether this socket was the tracked one

choice.doc:name: Clear current socket id if present
choice.doc:description: Clear the tracked socket only if it was this one

when.doc:description: This closed socket was the tracked one, so clear the stored socket id

os:contains.doc:name: Was HA-active?
os:contains.doc:description: Did this instance hold the stream

when.doc:description: We held the stream, so clear the flag to re-arm the failover poller

os:contains.doc:name: Was a takeover connect inflight?
os:contains.doc:description: Did this socket belong to a takeover attempt that never reached connection_ack

when.doc:description: The takeover's connect socket died before its ack could subscribe, so clear the marker

os:remove.doc:name: Clear ha-takeover-inflight
os:remove.doc:description: Stale after socket drop; the next poll tick will reschedule the takeover

choice.doc:name: Update lastConnectedAt if record exists
choice.doc:description: Record when the connection last dropped

when.doc:description: A saved Offset exists, so stamp it with the disconnect time

choice.doc:name: Route by close code (originalCode)
choice.doc:description: Classify why the socket closed; only 4403 changes app state

when.doc:description: Chain not enabled / bad chainCode - a config problem, so halt and stop probing

logger.doc:name: Log 4403 halt
logger.doc:description: Log a fatal configuration error and stop retrying
logger.message: Chain not enabled / bad chainCode (4403) - halting, this is a configuration problem. Not retrying.

os:store.doc:name: Set halted flag
os:store.doc:description: Stop the controller from probing again until restarted/redeployed with a fixed config

when.doc:description: Another consumer holds the lock; log only, controller re-probes and sees Active

logger.doc:name: Log 4409
logger.doc:description: Log that another consumer already holds the connection
logger.message: Single-Consumer Lock held (4409); controller will see Active and stand down on its next re-probe.

when.doc:description: Token expired/invalid; log only, connect-subflow fetches a fresh token on the next probe

logger.doc:name: Log 4401
logger.message: Token expired/invalid (4401); controller will fetch a fresh token on its next probe/reconnect.

logger.doc:name: Log routine close
logger.doc:description: Log any other close code (4504, 1006, 1000/1001, etc.)
logger.message: Close code #[vars.closeCode]; controller re-probes on its next poll tick.
